requirements.statement: Validate the server-owned ordered WorkflowDefinition and every StageDefinition, contract reference, harness reference, policy, and hash before use.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 1; accepted Design coverage C1, C2, C24, C31.

requirements.statement: Create or update no pull request during QRSPI stage work.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 10; accepted Design coverage C11, C16, C31, C44-C45.

requirements.statement: Prove registration, extension, skip, success, retry, restart, stale Generation, version recovery, uncertain publication, document revision, and implementation checkpoint handoff behavior.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 11; accepted Design coverage C41-C44 and impact verification V1-V11.

requirements.statement: Register Questions, Research, Design, Structure, Plan, and Implementation in deterministic order; an explicitly disabled stage creates no run.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 2; accepted Design coverage C3, C5, C15, C20-C23.

requirements.statement: Permit a built-in stage to be added through a contract and registration only, without a per-stage queue, worker, store family, Context tag, or central stage switch.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 3; accepted Design coverage C3, C4, C10, C30, C44.

requirements.statement: Use stable semantic type names and represent versions only as data.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 4; accepted Design coverage C1, C3, C6.

requirements.statement: Reject unknown, duplicate, unavailable, or incompatible versions before claim and retain versions referenced by active work across restart.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 5; accepted Design coverage C2, C24, C29, C33-C36, C44.

requirements.statement: Provide bounded Schema-decoded exact accepted source input while preserving ticket authority and technical-artifact precedence.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 6; accepted Design coverage C3, C7, C9, C44.

requirements.statement: Execute through the selected AgentHarness without giving the harness authority to advance StageRun or publish Git state.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 7; accepted Design coverage C8, C10, C28.

requirements.statement: Publish signed exact-parent document artifacts with exact-old branch updates and authoritative duplicate or uncertain-effect recovery.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 8; accepted Design coverage C11-C14, C29, C41.

requirements.statement: Release successors from accepted revisions only and prevent stale Generation, revision, session, review, handoff, publication, promotion, or reentry outcomes from advancing work.
requirements.description: Ticket acceptance criterion 9; accepted Design coverage C5-C7, C14-C17, C22-C25, C28-C29, C37.

resolutions.title: Trusted definitions and catalog
resolutions.position: Use server-owned hashed definitions, trusted versioned contracts and harnesses, one validator, and one erased catalog seam.
resolutions.rationale: Reject unsafe, unknown, incompatible, or unavailable selections before claim without repository code execution or per-stage branches.

inputs.summary: D1 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.made_by: Design producer via accepted Design revision 4

resolutions.title: Single runtime composition
resolutions.position: Add one catalog, store, service, publisher, and loop; fail only QRSPI closed; retain local diagnostics without a status or readiness product.
resolutions.rationale: Deploy the stage runtime without coupling unrelated service or taking operational-status ownership.

inputs.summary: D10 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.made_by: Design producer via accepted Design revision 4

resolutions.title: Legacy migration and offline recovery
resolutions.position: Preserve legacy rows, never infer facts, and require preflight manifest, verified backup, append-only apply or rollback, exact offline supersession, verification, then ordinary successor kickoff.
resolutions.rationale: Upgrade safely from shipped but unexecutable legacy child rows.

inputs.summary: D11 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.made_by: Design producer via accepted Design revision 4

resolutions.title: Lowest-boundary behavioral verification
resolutions.position: Use real SQLite and Git plus fault injection to prove reconciliation, diagnostics, owner availability and recovery, upgrade recovery, and the full stage matrix.
resolutions.rationale: Produce observable evidence at the lowest reliable boundaries.

inputs.summary: D12 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.made_by: Design producer via accepted Design revision 4

resolutions.title: No aggregate capacity subsystem
resolutions.position: Retain v1 audit records and nonterminal custody with individual bounds, but add no aggregate capacity policy, status, recovery, deletion, or invented owner.
resolutions.rationale: Preserve audit and recovery semantics without adding work outside ticket authority; R9 remains accepted residual risk.

inputs.summary: D13 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.title: Six exact typed stage contracts
resolutions.position: Use six distinct typed contracts with bounded immutable authority-ordered source sets and persisted exact request hashes.
resolutions.rationale: Preserve correct stage meaning, replay, and ticket authority.

inputs.summary: D2 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.made_by: Design producer via accepted Design revision 4

resolutions.title: Durable tagged runtime model
resolutions.position: Use strict tagged records and one generic operation lifecycle for leases, state, revision pointers, diagnostics, currentness, and restart.
resolutions.rationale: Provide durable progression without stale or malformed advancement.

inputs.summary: D3 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.made_by: Design producer via accepted Design revision 4

resolutions.title: Linear production and custody handoff
resolutions.position: Use linear initialization, session checkpoints, attempt workspaces, result validation, atomic custody transfer, and cleanup fencing.
resolutions.rationale: Keep agent execution safe and recoverable while denying the harness publication authority.

inputs.summary: D4 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.made_by: Design producer via accepted Design revision 4

resolutions.title: Exact artifact publication
resolutions.position: Verify exact scope, tree, and content; create one signed sole-parent commit; persist intent; update exact-old fast-forward-only; observe authoritatively.
resolutions.rationale: Protect immutable canonical artifacts from duplicate or unauthorized Git effects.

inputs.summary: D5 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.made_by: Design producer via accepted Design revision 4

resolutions.title: Publication-scoped reconciliation
resolutions.position: Route publication conflicts, stale effects, rollback, and ambiguous observations to one durable read-only reconciliation with exact typed resolutions.
resolutions.rationale: Reconcile external Git truth without stale parent advance or destructive mutation.

resolutions.position: Use accepted-only linear progression, distinct document and implementation shapes, observed contiguous commits, monotonic replacement revisions, and no stage pull request.
resolutions.rationale: Provide deterministic stage completion and checkpoint handoff.

resolutions.rationale: Provide available duplicate-safe cross-owner delivery without taking owner lifecycles.

inputs.summary: R9 accepted from Design revision 4 package bac9e02e7016aa3135b5cd0913003b6fe10d2ed8ece8f9c39a695e6e3d13da43

resolutions.made_by: Impact reviewer and synthesizer; final disposition by accountable human

rules.name: Validate complete activation inputs
rules.statement: Resolve and validate complete definitions, contracts, harnesses, policies, availability, hashes, paths, bounds, and retained active versions at every activation and restart boundary; fail QRSPI closed before claim with exact error.

rules.name: Validate mandatory owner capabilities
rules.statement: Derive mandatory owner references and validate registrations and availability before ingress, each activation, and new effects while unrelated service remains available.

rules.name: Recover the same handoff
rules.statement: Persist exact handoff diagnostics and observe or resubmit the same deterministic local receipt after failure, restart, and restoration; duplicates return the same result and mismatches remain blocked.

rules.name: Accept exact owner results only
rules.statement: Accept only exact typed owner results bound to current scope, package, policy, and request; approval alone and partial or uncertain results cannot release Structure.

rules.name: Require owner-side exact lifecycle
rules.statement: The selected named downstream owner must implement an idempotent owner-side lifecycle and exact result production behind the registered capability before its path is exposed.

rules.name: Pin Design and reentry identities
rules.statement: Pin all Design, promotion, and Structure identities; require the exact workflowd-vs3.9 result and snapshot for release; accept bounded workflowd-vs3.14 reentry and apply only named local effects idempotently.

rules.name: Classify legacy state before writes
rules.statement: Before normal startup writes, take a read-only snapshot and classify every current Generation and nonterminal operation into a canonical bounded manifest with exact diagnostics and actions.

rules.name: Back up and apply append-only
rules.statement: Verify unchanged manifest and database, fsync and verify same-filesystem database, WAL, and SHM backup, apply append-only schema transactionally, and prove rollback or restore and verify backup.

rules.name: Supersede exact no-effect legacy work
rules.statement: Resolve only exact no-effect legacy Generation through idempotent offline supersession, verify the result, and use ordinary authenticated WorkflowStart for the successor.

rules.name: Bound individual items without capacity claim
rules.statement: Enforce every configured and global payload and diff bound and release only workspace custody proven terminal or superseded; do not represent these as aggregate-capacity control.

rules.name: Bound exact semantic inputs
rules.statement: Decode and bound exact immutable source, request, and result bytes at the selected contract, preserve authority order, and reject hash, duplicate, or path mismatch before persistence or publication.

rules.name: Fence harness execution
rules.statement: Keep the trusted harness limited to task and session work; persist launch before create and session before prompt; bind attempt workspace and output to exact lease and session identity.

rules.name: Guard every transition
rules.statement: Guard every durable transition and external intent by exact Generation, run, revision, operation, attempt, session, and handoff identities; quarantine data errors and preserve stale audit.

rules.name: Fence cleanup and custody
rules.statement: Fence cleanup and workspace custody; permit no replacement while cleanup is unconfirmed and no deletion while publication or effect is nonterminal or uncertain.

rules.name: Verify exact publication candidate
rules.statement: Verify custody, scope, diff, path, content, parent, signature and trailers, and one final SHA, then use exact-old fast-forward-only mutation.

rules.name: Observe publication authoritatively
rules.statement: Persist intent before mutation and authoritatively observe remote ref, parent, signature, trailers, attribution, blob, and content before completion; observe an unknown effect before retry.

rules.name: Reconcile without destructive mutation
rules.statement: Atomically create one publication-scoped TargetReconcile, save parent state, make publication unclaimable, and permit only read-only observation and exact typed resolution.

rules.name: Retain reconciliation evidence
rules.statement: Retain complete directly queryable reconciliation identity, observations, error, allowed actions, and terminal resolution exactly once across restart.

rules.name: Verify activation validation
rules.statement: Prove complete config, catalog, and handoff validation prevents claim at the Layer and activation boundary.

rules.name: Verify individual bounds without capacity claim
rules.statement: Prove individual bounds and custody rules work while making no aggregate-capacity guarantee.

rules.name: Verify exact Design progression identities
rules.statement: Prove only exact current package, response, promotion, snapshot, and directive identities can alter Design progression or reentry.

rules.name: Verify exact bounded data
rules.statement: Prove exact source and result Schemas, hashes, bounds, order, and authority survive persistence and retry.

rules.name: Verify attempt and custody fencing
rules.statement: Prove session and workspace output transfer custody only under the exact current attempt and cleanup fence.

rules.name: Verify exact Git reconciliation
rules.statement: Prove publication and reconciliation preserve exact Git truth across every mutation, transaction, and restart window.

rules.name: Verify direct reconciliation recovery
rules.statement: Prove reconciliation remains directly recoverable without a status product.

rules.name: Verify capability availability
rules.statement: Prove mandatory owner capability exists before any configured crossing can create an effect.

rules.name: Verify same-handoff recovery
rules.statement: Prove an unavailable owner crossing recovers through one exact handoff and owner lifecycle.

rules.name: Verify upgrade safety
rules.statement: Prove upgrade classifies before writes, never converts or invents legacy state, and restores or proves the prior database on failure.

rules.name: Verify exact legacy retirement
rules.statement: Prove shipped recovery retires only exact dormant legacy work and creates a successor only through ordinary ingress.
